\begin{abstract}
This document presents the complete mathematical formalization of the KID-Calculus (Kondensation-Information-Dichte) as the foundation for Ontological Condensation theory. We define all variables with units and measurement procedures, formalize relationships to Assembly Index, Integrated Information Theory (IIT), Free Energy Principle (FEP), and derive the ``Toast-Heuristic'' from Prigogine's dissipative structures. Boundary conditions, critical thresholds, and a unified mathematical framework are established.
\end{abstract}

\tableofcontents
\newpage

%===============================================================================
\section{Foundational Definitions}
%===============================================================================

\begin{definition}[Ontological Condensation]
Ontological condensation $\mathcal{K}$ is the process by which information transitions from a state of maximum entropy (uniform probability distribution) to structured, causally effective configurations:
\begin{equation}
\mathcal{K}: \mathcal{H}_{max} \rightarrow \mathcal{H}_{structured}
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{H}_{max}$ represents the maximum entropy state and $\mathcal{H}_{structured}$ represents a lower-entropy, structured state.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[KID --- Kondensation-Information-Dichte]
The KID measure quantifies the density of causally effective information within an interaction volume over time:
\begin{equation}
KID = \oint_{\partial\Omega} \frac{I_{causal}}{V_{interaction}} \, dt
\end{equation}
\end{definition}

\subsection{Variable Definitions with Units}

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{@{}llll@{}}
\toprule
\textbf{Symbol} & \textbf{Meaning} & \textbf{Units} & \textbf{Measurement} \\
\midrule
$KID$ & Kondensation-Information-Dichte & bits$\cdot$s$^{-1}\cdot$m$^{-3}$ & Derived \\
$I_{causal}$ & Causally effective information & bits & Mutual information \\
$V_{interaction}$ & Volume of causal interaction & m$^3$ & Spatial correlation \\
$t$ & Time & s & Clock time \\
$\partial\Omega$ & Closed boundary of system & --- & System definition \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\caption{Variable definitions for the KID equation}
\end{table}

\subsection{Dimensional Analysis}
\begin{equation}
[KID] = \frac{[I_{causal}]}{[V_{interaction}]} \cdot [t] = \frac{\text{bits}}{\text{m}^3} \cdot \text{s} = \text{bits} \cdot \text{s} \cdot \text{m}^{-3}
\end{equation}

For time-integrated KID over duration $\tau$:
\begin{equation}
[KID_{integrated}] = \text{bits} \cdot \text{m}^{-3}
\end{equation}

\begin{definition}[Causal Information $I_{causal}$]
Causal information is defined as the mutual information between past and future states, weighted by causal efficacy:
\begin{equation}
I_{causal}(X) = I(X_{t}; X_{t+\Delta t}) \cdot \eta_{cause}
\end{equation}
where:
\begin{align}
I(X_{t}; X_{t+\Delta t}) &= H(X_{t}) + H(X_{t+\Delta t}) - H(X_{t}, X_{t+\Delta t}) \\
\eta_{cause} &= \frac{P(X_{t+\Delta t} | do(X_{t}))}{P(X_{t+\Delta t})}
\end{align}
\textit{Measurement: Via intervention studies or Granger causality in time series.}
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[Interaction Volume $V_{interaction}$]
The volume within which causal correlations are significant:
\begin{equation}
V_{interaction} = \int_{\mathbb{R}^3} \Theta(|C(r)| - \epsilon) \, d^3r
\end{equation}
where $C(r)$ is the spatial correlation function and $\epsilon$ is a threshold (typically $\epsilon = e^{-1}$ of maximum correlation).
\end{definition}

%===============================================================================
\section{Axioms of KID-Calculus}
%===============================================================================

\begin{axiom}[Information Fundamentalism]
Information is the fundamental ontological substrate:
\begin{equation}
\forall x \in \mathcal{O}: \exists I(x) \geq 0
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{O}$ is the set of all ontological entities.
\end{axiom}

\begin{axiom}[Condensation Irreversibility]
Ontological condensation is thermodynamically irreversible:
\begin{equation}
\mathcal{K}: \mathcal{H}_i \rightarrow \mathcal{H}_f \Rightarrow S_{total}(\mathcal{H}_f) > S_{total}(\mathcal{H}_i)
\end{equation}
where $S_{total}$ includes system + environment.
\end{axiom}

\begin{axiom}[Landauer Bound]
Every condensation step has a minimum energy cost:
\begin{equation}
\Delta E_{min} = k_B T \ln 2 \cdot \Delta I
\end{equation}
\end{axiom}

\begin{axiom}[Path Dependence]
The state of a condensed system depends on its history:
\begin{equation}
\Psi(t) = \mathcal{F}[\{\mathcal{K}(t')\}_{t' < t}]
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{F}$ is a functional of the condensation history.
\end{axiom}

\begin{axiom}[Emergence]
New ontological levels emerge at critical KID thresholds:
\begin{equation}
KID > \mathcal{E}_n \Rightarrow \text{Level } n+1 \text{ emerges}
\end{equation}
\end{axiom}

%===============================================================================
\section{Assembly Index Integration}
%===============================================================================

\begin{definition}[Assembly Index --- AI]
The Assembly Index $AI(x)$ of an object $x$ is the minimum number of condensation steps required to construct $x$ from elementary building blocks:
\begin{equation}
AI(x) = \min_{\gamma \in \Gamma(x)} |\gamma|
\end{equation}
where $\Gamma(x)$ is the set of all valid assembly pathways for $x$.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[KID-AI Relationship]
For any condensed object $x$, the KID is bounded below by the Assembly Index:
\begin{equation}
KID(x) \geq \frac{AI(x) \cdot I_{min}}{V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}
\end{equation}
where $I_{min} = k_B T \ln 2$ (Landauer limit per bit).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Each condensation step requires at least $I_{min}$ of information processing. For $AI(x)$ steps, at least $AI(x) \cdot I_{min}$ information is required, distributed over volume $V_{eff}$ and time $\tau_{form}$.
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}[AI as Path-Integrated KID]
The Assembly Index can be expressed as a path integral of KID:
\begin{equation}
AI(x) = \frac{1}{I_{min}} \int_{\gamma^*} KID(\vec{r}, t) \cdot V_{eff} \, dt
\end{equation}
where $\gamma^*$ is the minimal KID pathway.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[AI-KID Duality]
Assembly Index and KID are dual measures:
\begin{equation}
AI(x) \cdot KID(x) = \frac{I_{total}^2(x)}{I_{step} \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}
\end{equation}
\end{theorem}

%===============================================================================
\section{The Toast-Heuristic: From Prigogine to Complexity}
%===============================================================================

\begin{axiom}[Prigogine's Entropy Production]
For any dissipative structure, the total entropy production is:
\begin{equation}
\frac{dS_{total}}{dt} = \frac{dS_{system}}{dt} + \frac{dS_{environment}}{dt} \geq 0
\end{equation}
where local entropy reduction in the system is compensated by entropy export to the environment.
\end{axiom}

\begin{definition}[Dissipative Structure]
A dissipative structure $\mathcal{D}$ is a self-organizing system that maintains its order through continuous energy/matter flow:
\begin{equation}
\mathcal{D} = \{X \in \mathcal{X} : \frac{dS_{system}}{dt} < 0 \text{ and } \frac{dS_{total}}{dt} \geq 0\}
\end{equation}
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Toast-Heuristic: Complexity from Dissipation]
The complexity $C$ of a dissipative structure is proportional to its entropy production rate:
\begin{equation}
C(\mathcal{D}) = \alpha \cdot \frac{dS_{total}}{dt} \cdot \tau_{coherence}
\end{equation}
where $\alpha$ is a system-specific constant and $\tau_{coherence}$ is the structural coherence time.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
From Prigogine's minimum entropy production principle: $\frac{dS_{total}}{dt} = \int_V \sigma \, dV$ where $\sigma$ is the local entropy production density. For dissipative structures, order emerges when $\sigma_{internal} < 0$ and $\sigma_{export} > |\sigma_{internal}|$. The amount of structure is proportional to the exported entropy: $S_{structure} \propto \int_0^{\tau} |\sigma_{internal}| \, dt$. Complexity requires both structure and stability: $C = S_{structure} \cdot f(\tau_{coherence})$ where $f(\tau_{coherence}) = 1 - e^{-\tau_{coherence}/\tau_{char}}$.
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}[Toast Number $\mathcal{T}$]
The ``Toast Number'' quantifies the ``brownedness'' of a system:
\begin{equation}
\mathcal{T}(x) = \frac{KID(x)}{KID_{max}} \cdot \frac{\tau_{age}}{\tau_{relax}}
\end{equation}
\textit{Interpretation: $\mathcal{T} \ll 1$ (untoasted), $\mathcal{T} \sim 1$ (golden brown), $\mathcal{T} \gg 1$ (burnt).}
\end{definition}

\begin{corollary}[Optimal Toast Condition]
Maximum sustainable complexity occurs at:
\begin{equation}
\mathcal{T}_{optimal} = 1 \pm \delta
\end{equation}
where $\delta$ is system-dependent (typically $\delta \approx 0.3$). This corresponds to the ``edge of chaos.''
\end{corollary}

%===============================================================================
\section{Boundary Conditions and Critical Thresholds}
%===============================================================================

\begin{axiom}[Minimum Condensation Principle]
Condensation occurs only when KID exceeds a critical threshold:
\begin{equation}
KID > KID_{crit} = \frac{k_B T \ln 2}{V_{min} \cdot \tau_{fluct}}
\end{equation}
\end{axiom}

\begin{definition}[Condensation Threshold]
The critical KID for phase transition to condensed state:
\begin{equation}
KID_{crit}(T) = \frac{k_B T \ln 2}{\xi^d \cdot \tau_{cor}}
\end{equation}
where $\xi$ is the correlation length, $d$ is the spatial dimension, and $\tau_{cor}$ is the correlation time.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Phase Transition Condition]
A system undergoes ontological condensation when:
\begin{equation}
\frac{KID}{KID_{crit}} = \frac{\xi^d \cdot \tau_{cor} \cdot I_{causal}}{V_{interaction} \cdot k_B T \ln 2 \cdot \tau_{form}} > 1
\end{equation}
\end{theorem}

\begin{definition}[SOC Parameter $\mathcal{S}$]
The Self-Organized Criticality parameter measures proximity to criticality:
\begin{equation}
\mathcal{S} = \frac{KID - KID_{crit}}{KID_{sat} - KID_{crit}}
\end{equation}
\textit{Interpretation: $\mathcal{S} < 0$ (sub-critical), $\mathcal{S} = 0$ (critical), $0 < \mathcal{S} < 1$ (super-critical), $\mathcal{S} \rightarrow 1$ (saturation).}
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Emergence Criterion]
A new ontological level $n+1$ emerges when:
\begin{equation}
\int_{\Omega} KID \, dV \, dt \geq \mathcal{E}_{n+1} \quad \text{AND} \quad AI \geq AI_{min}^{(n+1)}
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{E}_n = n \cdot KID_{crit} \cdot \ln(AI_{min}^{(n)})$.
\end{theorem}

%===============================================================================
\section{Unified Framework: Connections to Existing Theories}
%===============================================================================

\subsection{KID and IIT ($\Phi$)}

\begin{theorem}[KID-$\Phi$ Equivalence]
For conscious systems, KID and $\Phi$ are related by:
\begin{equation}
\Phi = KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{int} \cdot \eta_{integration}
\end{equation}
\end{theorem}

\begin{corollary}[Consciousness Threshold]
Consciousness emerges when:
\begin{equation}
\Phi \geq \Phi_{min} \approx 0.5 \text{ bits} \iff KID \geq \frac{0.5}{V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{int} \cdot \eta_{integration}}
\end{equation}
\end{corollary}

\subsection{KID and FEP}

\begin{theorem}[KID-Free Energy Relationship]
The free energy is the thermodynamic potential of KID:
\begin{equation}
F = k_B T \cdot KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{process}
\end{equation}
\end{theorem}

\subsection{KID Master Equation}

\begin{theorem}[KID Master Equation]
All frameworks converge in the KID formalism:
\begin{equation}
KID = \frac{\Phi}{V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{int}} = \frac{F}{k_B T \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau} = \frac{AI \cdot I_{step}}{\int V_{eff} \, dt}
\end{equation}
\end{theorem}

\begin{corollary}[Ontological Condensation Principle]
All complex structures in the universe are manifestations of Kondensation-Information-Dichte exceeding critical thresholds:
\begin{equation}
\text{Complexity exists} \iff KID > KID_{crit}
\end{equation}
\end{corollary}

%===============================================================================
\section{Measurement Protocols}
%===============================================================================

\begin{protocol}[Measuring $I_{causal}$]
\begin{enumerate}
\item Record time series of system states: $\{X_t\}_{t=0}^{T}$
\item Compute mutual information: $I(X_t; X_{t+\Delta t})$
\item Estimate causal effect via intervention or Granger causality
\item Calculate: $I_{causal} = I(X_t; X_{t+\Delta t}) \cdot \eta_{cause}$
\end{enumerate}
\end{protocol}

\begin{protocol}[Measuring KID]
\begin{enumerate}
\item Measure $I_{causal}$ via Protocol 1
\item Measure $V_{interaction}$ via spatial correlation analysis
\item Measure formation time $\tau_{form}$
\item Calculate: $KID = \frac{I_{causal}}{V_{interaction} \cdot \tau_{form}}$
\end{enumerate}
\end{protocol}
